Scientific Institutions: Location Data & ZIP-Code Distance Pipeline

Author

Jan Pfänder

Published

May 10, 2026

Overview

This document prepares the dist_inst_km variable used in the alienation–trust preregistration: the geodesic distance (km) from each participant’s ZIP code to the nearest scientific institution.

What counts as a scientific institution?

We include two categories:

  1. Research universities — Carnegie R1 (Doctoral: Very High Research Activity) and R2 (Doctoral: High Research Activity) institutions, sourced from the Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS) via the Urban Institute’s educationdata package.

  2. Major federal research labs — approximately 50 facilities operated by DOE, NIH, NIST, NOAA, NASA, and USGS, with coordinates sourced from each agency’s website and verified against OpenStreetMap.

Why log-transform? Geographic distance is right-skewed (a few very remote participants drive the distribution). All analyses use log(dist_inst_km).

Outputs (saved to data/institutions/, gitignored):

  • institutions.rds — clean institution dataset with coordinates
  • zipcode_distances.rds — ZIP-code lookup table: one row per ZIP, with nearest institution and distance in km

Setup

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(sf)
library(geosphere)
library(zipcodeR)
Code 
dir.create(here("data", "institutions"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

Part 1: Research Universities (IPEDS)

We download the IPEDS Header/Directory file (HD2022) directly from NCES. This file is the authoritative source for institution characteristics including coordinates and Carnegie classification codes. Downloading directly avoids any API dependency.

Carnegie classification codes of interest (variable C18BASIC in IPEDS):

Code Label
15 Doctoral Universities: Very High Research Activity (R1)
16 Doctoral Universities: High Research Activity (R2)
Code 
# Download IPEDS HD2022 (Header/Directory) — ~3 MB zip, ~6,000 institutions.
# Cached by knitr after first run.
ipeds_zip  <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip")
ipeds_dir  <- tempdir()

download.file(
  url      = "https://nces.ed.gov/ipeds/datacenter/data/HD2022.zip",
  destfile = ipeds_zip,
  quiet    = TRUE,
  mode     = "wb"
)

unzip(ipeds_zip, exdir = ipeds_dir)

# The main CSV is lowercase in the zip; find it regardless of case
csv_file   <- list.files(ipeds_dir, pattern = "(?i)^hd2022\\.csv$",
                         full.names = TRUE)[1]
ipeds_raw  <- read_csv(csv_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Key columns: INSTNM, CITY, STABBR, LATITUDE, LONGITUD, C18BASIC
glimpse(ipeds_raw |> select(INSTNM, CITY, STABBR, LATITUDE, LONGITUD, C18BASIC))
Rows: 6,256
Columns: 6
$ INSTNM   <chr> "Alabama A & M University", "University of Alabama at Birming…
$ CITY     <chr> "Normal", "Birmingham", "Montgomery", "Huntsville", "Montgome…
$ STABBR   <chr> "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "AL", "…
$ LATITUDE <dbl> 34.78337, 33.50570, 32.36261, 34.72456, 32.36432, 33.20701, 3…
$ LONGITUD <dbl> -86.56850, -86.79935, -86.17401, -86.64045, -86.29568, -87.52…
$ C18BASIC <dbl> 18, 15, 20, 16, 19, -2, 15, 2, 22, 18, 15, 21, 1, 20, 5, 5, 1…
Code 
universities <- ipeds_raw |>
  filter(
    C18BASIC %in% c(15, 16),        # R1 and R2 only
    !is.na(LATITUDE),
    !is.na(LONGITUD),
    # Drop non-contiguous US (Alaska, Hawaii, territories)
    LATITUDE  > 24 & LATITUDE  < 50,
    LONGITUD  > -125 & LONGITUD < -66
  ) |>
  transmute(
    name             = INSTNM,
    city             = CITY,
    state_abbr       = STABBR,
    lat              = LATITUDE,
    lon              = LONGITUD,
    carnegie_class   = if_else(C18BASIC == 15, "R1", "R2"),
    institution_type = "university"
  )

cat("R1 universities:", sum(universities$carnegie_class == "R1"), "\n")
R1 universities: 131
Code 
cat("R2 universities:", sum(universities$carnegie_class == "R2"), "\n")
R2 universities: 131
Code 
cat("Total:          ", nrow(universities), "\n")
Total:           262
Code 
universities_sf <- st_as_sf(universities, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = universities_sf,
          aes(color = carnegie_class), size = 1.2, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(R1 = "#2c7bb6", R2 = "#abd9e9"),
                     name = "Carnegie") +
  labs(title = "Research universities included (R1 + R2)",
       caption = "Source: IPEDS 2022 via Urban Institute educationdata package") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

Part 2: Federal Research Labs

Manually curated list of ~50 major federal research facilities. Coordinates verified against agency websites and OpenStreetMap. This list intentionally covers the largest, most prominent labs — it is not exhaustive of every federal facility, but captures the institutions most relevant to structural proximity to organized science.

Code 
federal_labs <- tribble(
  ~name,                                          ~agency,  ~lat,     ~lon,
  # ── Department of Energy national labs ─────────────────────────────────
  "Argonne National Laboratory",                  "DOE",   41.7059, -87.9837,
  "Brookhaven National Laboratory",               "DOE",   40.8674, -72.8776,
  "Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory",        "DOE",   41.8319, -88.2617,
  "Idaho National Laboratory",                    "DOE",   43.5246,-112.9541,
  "Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory",        "DOE",   37.8755,-122.2477,
  "Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory",       "DOE",   37.6868,-121.7051,
  "Los Alamos National Laboratory",               "DOE",   35.8800,-106.2951,
  "National Renewable Energy Laboratory",         "DOE",   39.7405,-105.1686,
  "Oak Ridge National Laboratory",                "DOE",   35.9310, -84.3100,
  "Pacific Northwest National Laboratory",        "DOE",   46.3440,-119.2785,
  "Sandia National Laboratories (NM)",            "DOE",   34.9653,-106.3969,
  "Sandia National Laboratories (CA)",            "DOE",   37.9717,-121.7046,
  "SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory",         "DOE",   37.4160,-122.2034,
  "Ames National Laboratory",                     "DOE",   42.0267, -93.7317,
  "Jefferson Lab",                                "DOE",   37.0977, -76.4791,
  "Savannah River National Laboratory",           "DOE",   33.3487, -81.7229,
  "National Energy Technology Laboratory",        "DOE",   39.9279, -79.9701,
  # ── National Institutes of Health ──────────────────────────────────────
  "NIH Main Campus",                              "NIH",   39.0007, -77.1055,
  "NIH Rocky Mountain Labs",                      "NIH",   46.8574,-113.9867,
  "NIH Research Triangle Park",                   "NIH",   35.8973, -78.8622,
  # ── National Institute of Standards and Technology ──────────────────────
  "NIST Gaithersburg",                            "NIST",  39.1330, -77.2177,
  "NIST Boulder",                                 "NIST",  40.0050,-105.2625,
  # ── NOAA research labs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "NOAA Earth System Research Lab (Boulder)",     "NOAA",  39.9950,-105.2639,
  "NOAA Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Lab",          "NOAA",  40.3502, -74.6573,
  "NOAA Atlantic Oceanographic Lab (Miami)",      "NOAA",  25.7306, -80.1625,
  "NOAA Pacific Marine Environmental Lab",        "NOAA",  47.6456,-122.3130,
  "NOAA National Severe Storms Lab",              "NOAA",  35.1817, -97.4432,
  "NOAA Great Lakes Environmental Research Lab",  "NOAA",  42.2986, -83.7082,
  # ── NASA field centers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "NASA Ames Research Center",                    "NASA",  37.4094,-122.0640,
  "NASA Armstrong Flight Research Center",        "NASA",  34.9594,-117.8822,
  "NASA Glenn Research Center",                   "NASA",  41.4273, -81.8633,
  "NASA Goddard Space Flight Center",             "NASA",  38.9915, -76.8404,
  "NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory",               "NASA",  34.2014,-118.1720,
  "NASA Johnson Space Center",                    "NASA",  29.5593, -95.0938,
  "NASA Kennedy Space Center",                    "NASA",  28.5729, -80.6490,
  "NASA Langley Research Center",                 "NASA",  37.0837, -76.3694,
  "NASA Marshall Space Flight Center",            "NASA",  34.7293, -86.6650,
  "NASA Stennis Space Center",                    "NASA",  30.3629, -89.6003,
  # ── USGS science centers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "USGS National Center (Reston)",                "USGS",  38.9476, -77.3673,
  "USGS Western Region (Menlo Park)",             "USGS",  37.4574,-122.1730,
  "USGS Central Region (Denver)",                 "USGS",  39.7543,-104.8797,
  "USGS Woods Hole Coastal Science Center",       "USGS",  41.5250, -70.6710,
  "USGS Columbia Environmental Research Center",  "USGS",  38.9387, -92.3341,
  "USGS Patuxent Wildlife Research Center",       "USGS",  38.9980, -76.8155,
  # ── CDC / EPA ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  "CDC / ATSDR (Atlanta)",                        "CDC",   33.7993, -84.3277,
  "EPA Office of Research & Development (RTP)",   "EPA",   35.8905, -78.8751,
  "EPA National Risk Management Lab (Cincinnati)","EPA",   39.1290, -84.5122
) |>
  mutate(institution_type = "federal_lab",
         carnegie_class   = NA_character_,
         city             = NA_character_,
         state_abbr       = NA_character_)

cat("Federal labs:", nrow(federal_labs), "\n")
Federal labs: 47
Code 
labs_sf <- st_as_sf(federal_labs, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = labs_sf, aes(color = agency), size = 2) +
  labs(title = "Federal research labs included",
       color = "Agency",
       caption = "Coordinates verified against agency websites and OpenStreetMap") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

Part 3: Combined Institution Dataset

Code 
institutions <- bind_rows(
  universities |> select(name, city, state_abbr, lat, lon,
                          carnegie_class, institution_type),
  federal_labs |> select(name, city, state_abbr, lat, lon,
                          carnegie_class, institution_type)
)

institutions |>
  count(institution_type, carnegie_class) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Institution counts by type")
Institution counts by type
institution_type carnegie_class n
federal_lab NA 47
university R1 131
university R2 131
Code 
inst_sf <- st_as_sf(institutions, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = inst_sf,
          aes(color = institution_type, size = institution_type),
          alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(university = "#2c7bb6", federal_lab = "#d7191c"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_size_manual(values  = c(university = 0.8, federal_lab = 2),
                    guide   = "none") +
  labs(title    = "All scientific institutions",
       subtitle = paste0(sum(institutions$institution_type == "university"),
                         " R1/R2 universities + ",
                         sum(institutions$institution_type == "federal_lab"),
                         " federal labs")) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
Code 
saveRDS(institutions, here("data", "institutions", "institutions.rds"))
cat("Saved institutions.rds:", nrow(institutions), "rows\n")
Saved institutions.rds: 309 rows

Part 4: ZIP Code Distance Pipeline

For each US ZIP code, we compute the geodesic distance (km) to the nearest institution using the Haversine formula. ZIP-code centroids come from zipcodeR::zip_code_db, which covers all ~40,000 US ZIP codes with no API calls required.

We restrict to the continental US (latitude 24°–50°, longitude −125° to −66°) to match the participant sample and the institution coverage above.

Code 
zips <- zip_code_db |>
  filter(
    !is.na(lat), !is.na(lng),
    lat  > 24 & lat  < 50,
    lng > -125 & lng < -66
  ) |>
  select(zip_code = zipcode, zip_lat = lat, zip_lon = lng)

cat("ZIP codes in continental US:", nrow(zips), "\n")
ZIP codes in continental US: 32657
Code 
# Convert to sf for fast nearest-neighbor search
zips_sf  <- st_as_sf(zips, coords = c("zip_lon", "zip_lat"), crs = 4326)
inst_sf2 <- st_as_sf(institutions, coords = c("lon", "lat"),  crs = 4326)

# Find index of nearest institution for each ZIP code
nearest_idx <- st_nearest_feature(zips_sf, inst_sf2)

# Retrieve institution metadata for each matched row
nearest_inst <- institutions[nearest_idx, ] |>
  select(nearest_institution = name, institution_type)

# Compute Haversine distance (km) between each ZIP centroid and its nearest institution
inst_coords <- cbind(institutions$lon[nearest_idx],
                     institutions$lat[nearest_idx])
zip_coords  <- cbind(zips$zip_lon, zips$zip_lat)

dist_km <- distHaversine(zip_coords, inst_coords) / 1000

zipcode_distances <- bind_cols(zips, nearest_inst) |>
  mutate(dist_inst_km = dist_km)

head(zipcode_distances)
  zip_code zip_lat zip_lon                 nearest_institution institution_type
1    35004   33.62  -86.50 University of Alabama at Birmingham       university
2    35005   33.59  -86.99 University of Alabama at Birmingham       university
3    35006   33.40  -87.20           The University of Alabama       university
4    35007   33.22  -86.79 University of Alabama at Birmingham       university
5    35010   32.90  -85.90                   Auburn University       university
6    35013   33.90  -86.40 University of Alabama at Birmingham       university
  dist_inst_km
1     30.54386
2     20.02362
3     38.40107
4     31.81551
5     51.04835
6     57.39679

Validation

Code 
summary(zipcode_distances$dist_inst_km)
    Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
  0.0291  28.7875  62.2344  77.5828 103.8492 581.0799
Code 
# NYC (10001) → should be near 0; rural Montana (59931) → should be large
zipcode_distances |>
  filter(zip_code %in% c("10001", "59931", "90210", "60601", "73301")) |>
  select(zip_code, nearest_institution, institution_type, dist_inst_km) |>
  mutate(dist_inst_km = round(dist_inst_km, 1)) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Spot checks: selected ZIP codes")
Spot checks: selected ZIP codes
zip_code nearest_institution institution_type dist_inst_km
90210 University of California-Los Angeles university 3.7
60601 DePaul University university 0.7
59931 The University of Montana university 119.5
10001 CUNY Graduate School and University Center university 0.5
Code 
ggplot(zipcode_distances, aes(x = dist_inst_km)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 80, fill = "#2c7bb6", color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    x        = "Distance to nearest institution (km, log scale)",
    y        = "Number of ZIP codes",
    title    = "Distribution of dist_inst_km across continental US ZIP codes",
    subtitle = "Right-skewed → log-transform used in analysis"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
Code 
saveRDS(zipcode_distances, here("data", "institutions", "zipcode_distances.rds"))
cat("Saved zipcode_distances.rds:", nrow(zipcode_distances), "rows\n")
Saved zipcode_distances.rds: 32657 rows
